name: program-assistant
description: Ask the Cornell Systems Engineering program assistant. Grounded, cited answers from the program's course roster, faculty directory, website, and official forms — powered by a Cornell-hosted n8n RAG workflow. Supports /name skills.
long_description: A thin Cowork/MCP front-end over the program's Retrieval-Augmented-Generation engine. The single tool `ask_program` sends a question to a header-authed n8n webhook on Cornell infrastructure, which runs hybrid retrieval over the curated program knowledge base and answers with inline [n] citations (declining when the answer isn't in the sources). No corpus or model API keys live in this plugin — only the webhook URL and a shared token. TWO ways to supply the token: this settings menu, OR a plain text file (~/.systemsbot/tokens.env) you edit by hand — no menus needed, see ONBOARDING.md. Supports named skills via `/skill-name` (e.g. `/who-to-contact`, `/course-planning`, `/forms-and-deadlines`).

rag_webhook_url.title: RAG webhook URL
rag_webhook_url.description: The n8n webhook that runs the program RAG, e.g. https://n8n-dev.lcmain.aaii.cucloud.net/webhook/rag-ask

rag_webhook_token.title: Webhook token (X-RAG-Token)
rag_webhook_token.description: Shared secret the webhook requires in the X-RAG-Token header. Get it from the program administrator. OPTIONAL here: you can instead paste it into a plain text file (~/.systemsbot/tokens.env) — see ONBOARDING.md. Leave this blank if you're using the file.

tools.name: ask_program
tools.description: Ask the Systems Engineering program assistant; returns a grounded, cited answer (+ sources). Supports /name skills.
